content:
# Numbers Chapter 27 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 181–182 | **Source Image:** `NUM_027_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_027_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. pa_0134–pa_0135

---

**1.** And the daughters of Zelophehad son of Hepher, son of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh, of the families of Manasseh son of Joseph, drew near — and these [are] the names of his daughters: Mahlah, Noah, and Hoglah, and Milcah, and Tirzah —

**2.** and they stood before Moses and before Eleazar the priest and before the leaders and all the congregation, [at] the opening of the tent of meeting, saying:

**3.** "Our father died in the wilderness, and he was not in the midst of the congregation, the ones who gathered themselves together against YHWH in the congregation of Korah, but in his [own] sin he died, and sons he did not have.

**4.** Why should the name of our father be withdrawn from the midst of his family because he had no son? Give to us a possession in the midst of the brothers of our father."

**5.** And Moses brought their judgment before YHWH.

**6.** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying:

**7.** "Rightly the daughters of Zelophehad speak. You shall surely give to them a possession of an inheritance in the midst of the brothers of their father, and you shall cause the inheritance of their father to pass over to them.

**8.** And to the sons of Israel you shall speak, saying: 'If a man dies and he has no son, then you shall cause his inheritance to pass over to his daughter.

**9.** And if he has no daughter, then you shall give his inheritance to his brothers.

**10.** And if he has no brothers, then you shall give his inheritance to the brothers of his father.

**11.** And if there are no brothers to his father, then you shall give his inheritance to his kinsman, the one nearest to him from his family, and he shall possess it.' And it shall be for the sons of Israel a statute of judgment, as YHWH commanded Moses."

**12.** And YHWH said to Moses: "Go up to this mountain of Abarim and see the land which I have given to the sons of Israel.

**13.** And you shall see it, and you shall be gathered to your people, also you, as Aaron your brother was gathered,

**14.** because you rebelled against my word in the wilderness of Zin, in the strife of the congregation, to sanctify me at the waters before their eyes." These [are] the waters of Meribah of Kadesh, [in] the wilderness of Zin.

**15.** And Moses spoke to YHWH, saying:

**16.** "Let YHWH, the God of the spirits of all flesh, appoint a man over the congregation,

**17.** who shall go out before them and who shall come in before them, and who shall lead them out and who shall bring them in, and the congregation of YHWH shall not be as sheep which have no shepherd."

**18.** And YHWH said to Moses: "Take to yourself Joshua son of Nun, a man in whom [is] spirit, and you shall lay your hand upon him.

**19.** And you shall stand him before Eleazar the priest and before all the congregation, and you shall commission him before their eyes.

**20.** And you shall put from your splendor upon him, so that all the congregation of the sons of Israel may hear [him].

**21.** And before Eleazar the priest he shall stand, and he shall inquire for him by the judgment of the Urim before YHWH. At his word they shall go out and at his word they shall come in, he and all the sons of Israel with him, and all the congregation."

**22.** And Moses did as YHWH commanded him. And he took Joshua and stood him before Eleazar the priest and before all the congregation.

**23.** And he laid his hands upon him and commissioned him, as YHWH spoke by the hand of Moses.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Verse 1 — Genealogy structure:** The Hebrew traces Zelophehad's lineage through five generations back to Joseph (צְלָפְחָד בֶּן־חֵפֶר בֶּן־גִּלְעָד בֶּן־מָכִיר בֶּן־מְנַשֶּׁה לְמִשְׁפְּחֹת מְנַשֶּׁה בֶן־יוֹסֵף). The phrase "of the families of Manasseh son of Joseph" (לְמִשְׁפְּחֹת) serves as a clan-level qualifier, distinguishing this branch within the broader tribe. The five daughters are named in this specific order in the Leningrad Codex: Mahlah (מַחְלָה), Noah (נֹעָה), Hoglah (חָגְלָה), Milcah (מִלְכָּה), and Tirzah (תִּרְצָה).

2. **Verse 3 — "In his own sin he died":** The Hebrew בְּחֶטְאוֹ מֵת is ambiguous. The daughters' argument is that their father was not among Korah's rebels (עֵדָה הַנּוֹעָדִים עַל־יְהוָה בַּעֲדַת־קֹרַח) but died of ordinary sin — i.e., the general decree of death upon the wilderness generation (cf. Num 14:35). The Vaticanus LXX reads similarly (ἐν τῇ ἁμαρτίᾳ αὐτοῦ ἀπέθανεν), confirming this reading.

3. **Verse 4 — "Be withdrawn" (יִגָּרַע):** The niphal of גָּרַע — "to be diminished, withdrawn, subtracted." The daughters argue that the absence of a male heir should not cause their father's name to disappear from his clan's land allotment.

4. **Verse 7 — Infinitive absolute:** The Hebrew כֵּן בְּנוֹת צְלָפְחָד דֹּבְרֹת opens with כֵּן ("rightly/correctly"). The participle דֹּבְרֹת ("speaking") is feminine plural, grammatically matching the daughters as subject. I have rendered this "Rightly the daughters of Zelophehad speak" to preserve the emphatic fronting of כֵּן. The verb נָתֹן תִּתֵּן ("you shall surely give") employs the infinitive absolute construction, preserved here with "surely give."

5. **Verse 11 — "Kinsman" (שְׁאֵרוֹ):** From שְׁאֵר, meaning "flesh, blood-relative, kinsman." The Leningrad Codex reads שְׁאֵרוֹ הַקָּרֹב אֵלָיו מִמִּשְׁפַּחְתּוֹ — "his kinsman, the one nearest to him from his family." This establishes the final tier of the inheritance law.

6. **Verse 12 — "Mountain of Abarim" (הַר הָעֲבָרִים):** Abarim is a mountain range east of the Jordan. The specific peak is later identified as Mount Nebo (Deut 32:49). The Vaticanus LXX renders this τὸ ὄρος τὸ ἐν τῷ Αβαριμ.

7. **Verse 14 — "Waters of Meribah of Kadesh":** The Hebrew מֵי־מְרִיבַת קָדֵשׁ מִדְבַּר־צִן. Meribah (מְרִיבָה) means "strife/contention." The full phrase locates the incident at Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin (cf. Num 20:1–13). The phrase "to sanctify me" (לְהַקְדִּישֵׁנִי) refers to the failure of Moses and Aaron to uphold YHWH's holiness before the people.

8. **Verse 16 — "God of the spirits of all flesh":** The Hebrew אֱלֹהֵי הָרוּחֹת לְכָל־בָּשָׂר is a distinctive divine epithet appearing also in Num 16:22. It emphasizes YHWH's sovereignty over the life-breath of every living creature.

9. **Verse 18 — "A man in whom is spirit":** The Hebrew אִישׁ אֲשֶׁר־רוּחַ בּוֹ. The noun רוּחַ here is unqualified — not "the spirit of YHWH" or "a holy spirit" but simply "spirit." This likely denotes the capacity for leadership and divine insight. The Vaticanus LXX reads ἄνθρωπον ὃς ἔχει πνεῦμα ἐν αὐτῷ, preserving the same ambiguity.

10. **Verse 20 — "Splendor" (הוֹד):** The Hebrew וְנָתַתָּה מֵהוֹדְךָ עָלָיו — "and you shall put from your splendor upon him." The noun הוֹד denotes majesty, splendor, or authority. Moses is to transfer a portion (מִן — partitive "from") of his authority to Joshua — not the entirety, which the Masoretic text carefully signals.

11. **Verse 21 — "Judgment of the Urim":** The Hebrew מִשְׁפַּט הָאוּרִים. The Urim (אוּרִים) is the oracular instrument used by the high priest for divine inquiry. The full expression "judgment of the Urim" (מִשְׁפַּט הָאוּרִים) occurs only here in the Torah, establishing that Joshua, unlike Moses, will require priestly mediation to receive divine guidance.

12. **Verse 23 — "Laid his hands" (plural):** The Leningrad Codex reads וַיִּסְמֹךְ אֶת־יָדָיו — "his hands" (dual/plural), whereas verse 18 commanded Moses to lay "your hand" (יָדְךָ, singular). This discrepancy is preserved in the translation. The Vaticanus LXX uses the plural τὰς χεῖρας αὐτοῦ, agreeing with the MT reading. Some commentators see Moses exceeding the command in generosity; others regard the singular in v. 18 as a generic expression.

— *EMET*
